entries.name: Half Moon Bay
entries.desc: A crescent-shaped bay on Antigua's remote southeastern coast, enclosed by rocky headlands, with a calmer reef-sheltered end and a more exposed, surf-prone stretch.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the crescent-shaped Half Moon Bay on Antigua's southeastern coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: half-moon-bay-antigua.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Half Moon bay and Exchange Bay, Antigua and Barbuda - panoramio" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Half_Moon_bay_and_Exchange_Bay,_Antigua_and_Barbuda_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Eagle Beach
entries.desc: A wide, low-density beach on Aruba's western coast, known for its soft white sand, gently shelving water, and the two weathered fofoti trees that stand at its edge.

photo.alt: A weathered fofoti tree leaning over the sand at Eagle Beach, Aruba
photo.credit: <!-- photo: fofoti-tree-eagle-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Fofoti - Eagle Beach Aruba (WoA) 08" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fofoti_-_Eagle_Beach_Aruba_(WoA)_08.jpg -->

entries.name: Whitehaven Beach
entries.desc: A beach on an uninhabited Whitsunday island known for exceptionally fine, bright silica sand and, at its northern end, the swirling sand-and-water patterns of Hill Inlet.

photo.alt: View over the fine white sand and swirling shallows of Whitehaven Beach, Whitsunday Island
photo.credit: <!-- photo: whitehaven-beach-01.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Whitsunday Island - Whitehaven Beach 01" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Whitsunday_Island_-_Whitehaven_Beach_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Turquoise Bay
entries.desc: A beach in Cape Range National Park on the Ningaloo Coast, where a fringing coral reef sits just offshore and can be reached by snorkelers straight from the sand.

photo.alt: Clear turquoise shallows and white sand at Turquoise Bay on the Ningaloo Coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: turquoise-bay-ningaloo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Turquoise Bay Bundera" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Turquoise_Bay_Bundera.jpg -->

entries.name: Bondi Beach
entries.desc: Sydney's best-known ocean beach, a wide sweep of sand in the eastern suburbs backed by a promenade and the start of the coastal walk to Coogee.

photo.alt: Wide aerial view of Bondi Beach's curved sand and promenade in Sydney's eastern suburbs
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bondi-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Bondi Beach" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bondi_Beach.jpg -->

entries.name: Hyams Beach
entries.desc: A small beach on the sheltered shore of Jervis Bay, noted for very fine, bright white sand and calm, clear water backed by bushland.

photo.alt: Fine white sand and clear turquoise water at Hyams Beach, framed by bushland, Jervis Bay
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hyams-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "2020-02-29 Hyams Beach, New South Wales" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:2020-02-29_Hyams_Beach,_New_South_Wales.jpg -->

entries.name: Cable Beach
entries.desc: A very long white and red-tinged sand beach on Broome's Indian Ocean coast, famous for camel rides at sunset and dramatic tidal flats that expose broad mudflats at low water.

photo.alt: Silhouetted camel train at sunset on Cable Beach, Broome
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cable-beach-camels-broome.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Cable Beach Sunset Camel Ride" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cable_Beach_Sunset_Camel_Ride.JPG -->

entries.name: Wineglass Bay
entries.desc: A curved white-sand beach in Freycinet National Park on Tasmania's east coast, reached by a 30-minute climb over a pink granite saddle, with clear water in a sheltered bay.

photo.alt: Curved white-sand Wineglass Bay seen from the granite saddle lookout, Freycinet National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: wineglass-bay-lookout.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Wineglass Bay from Lookout crop" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wineglass_Bay_from_Lookout_crop.jpg -->

entries.name: Pink Sands Beach
entries.desc: A long beach on the Atlantic side of Harbour Island whose sand carries a soft pink tint, produced by crushed red foraminifera shells mixed into the white coral sand.

photo.alt: Pink-tinted sand beach on Harbour Island, Bahamas
photo.credit: <!-- photo: pink-sand-beach-harbour-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Pink Sands Beach, Harbour Island, Bahamas (5279465868)" by pazzani · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pink_Sands_Beach,_Harbour_Island,_Bahamas_(5279465868).jpg -->

entries.name: Pig Beach (Big Major Cay)
entries.desc: A small sandy cove on an uninhabited Exuma cay, reached by boat and known for the feral pigs that wade and swim in its shallow turquoise water.

photo.alt: Feral pigs wading in shallow water on an Exuma cay
photo.credit: <!-- photo: pig-beach-exuma.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Pigs and gulls on the beach" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pigs_and_gulls_on_the_beach.jpg -->

entries.name: Sandy Lane Beach
entries.desc: A calm white-sand beach on the sheltered Caribbean west coast of Barbados, part of the upscale Platinum Coast, with clear water suited to swimming and snorkeling.

photo.alt: White-sand beach on Barbados's sheltered west coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sandy-lane-beach-barbados.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Sandy Lane" by Randy Storey · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sandy_Lane.jpg -->

entries.name: Baía do Sancho
entries.desc: A cliff-enclosed bay on the volcanic Fernando de Noronha archipelago, reached by ladders set into a rock crevice, with clear water and abundant marine life.

photo.alt: Cliff-enclosed bay of Baía do Sancho, Fernando de Noronha
photo.credit: <!-- photo: baia-do-sancho-fernando-de-noronha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Baia do Sancho Fernando de Noronha" by Roberta monte · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Baia_do_Sancho_Fernando_de_Noronha.JPG -->

entries.name: Baía dos Porcos
entries.desc: A tiny rock-pool beach on Fernando de Noronha, framed by dark volcanic rock and looking out to the archipelago's twin Dois Irmãos sea stacks.

photo.alt: Rock-pool cove at Baía dos Porcos, Fernando de Noronha
photo.credit: <!-- photo: baia-dos-porcos-fernando-de-noronha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Baía dos Porcos - Fernando de Noronha (32811749914)" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ba%C3%ADa_dos_Porcos_-_Fernando_de_Noronha_(32811749914).jpg -->

entries.name: Lopes Mendes
entries.desc: A long white-sand beach on the car-free island of Ilha Grande, backed by Atlantic rainforest and reached only by boat or a forest hiking trail.

photo.alt: White-sand beach backed by Atlantic rainforest on Ilha Grande
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lopes-mendes-beach-ilha-grande.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Praia de Lopes Mendes, Ilha Grande" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Praia_de_Lopes_Mendes,_Ilha_Grande.jpg -->

entries.name: Ipanema Beach
entries.desc: A wide golden-sand Atlantic beach in Rio's Zona Sul, divided informally into social zones and framed by the rocky headlands of Arpoador and Dois Irmãos at each end.

photo.alt: Golden-sand Ipanema Beach in Rio's Zona Sul
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ipanema-beach-rio-de-janeiro.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Ipanema beach and neighborhood - Rio de Janeiro Brazil (5269533104)" by David Berkowitz · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ipanema_beach_and_neighborhood_-_Rio_de_Janeiro_Brazil_(5269533104).jpg -->

entries.name: Koh Rong (Long Beach)
entries.desc: A long white-sand beach on Cambodia's largest island in the Gulf of Thailand, backed by jungle and known for calm water and bioluminescent plankton at night.

photo.alt: Long white-sand beach on Koh Rong, Gulf of Thailand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: long-beach-koh-rong-cambodia.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Long-beach-kog-rong-cambodia" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Long-beach-kog-rong-cambodia.jpg -->

entries.name: Seven Mile Beach
entries.desc: A coral-sand beach running along Grand Cayman's western shore, lined with resorts and condominiums and fronting calm, clear water protected from open-sea swells.

photo.alt: Seven Mile Beach, Grand Cayman, white sand fronting calm turquoise water with resorts along the shore
photo.credit: <!-- photo: seven-mile-beach-grand-cayman.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Resorts on Seven Mile Beach, Grand Cayman.jpg" by RoadTripWarrior · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Resorts_on_Seven_Mile_Beach,_Grand_Cayman.jpg -->

entries.name: Tayrona (Cabo San Juan)
entries.desc: A twin-cove beach within Tayrona National Park on Colombia's Caribbean coast, reached by a jungle trail, where boulders and forest meet the sea.

photo.alt: Cabo San Juan del Guía cove in Tayrona National Park, boulders and palms framing the beach
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cabo-san-juan-tayrona.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "El Cabo at Tayrona National Park.JPG" by Uhkabu · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:El_Cabo_at_Tayrona_National_Park.JPG -->

entries.name: Aitutaki Lagoon
entries.desc: A large triangular lagoon in the Cook Islands, dotted with small uninhabited islets (motus) and sandbars ringed by shallow, vividly coloured water.

photo.alt: Palm-lined shore of the Aitutaki lagoon, Cook Islands, with shallow turquoise water
photo.credit: <!-- photo: aitutaki-lagoon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Aitutaki-Motu Tapuaetai.jpg" by Emeseee · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aitutaki-Motu_Tapuaetai.jpg -->

entries.name: Manuel Antonio Beach
entries.desc: A calm Pacific beach bordering Manuel Antonio National Park, where rainforest meets the sand and monkeys, sloths, and iguanas are commonly seen among the trees.

photo.alt: Main beach at Manuel Antonio National Park, Costa Rica, rainforest meeting the sand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: manuel-antonio-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Main Beach Manuel Antonio National Park Costa Rica.JPG" by Eric T Gunther · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Main_Beach_Manuel_Antonio_National_Park_Costa_Rica.JPG -->

entries.name: Zlatni Rat (Golden Horn)
entries.desc: A distinctive white-pebble spit projecting from the island of Brač into the Adriatic, its tapering tip shifting shape with the prevailing winds and currents.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the tapering Zlatni Rat spit on Brač, Croatia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: zlatni-rat-brac.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Zlatni rat from Vidova Gora.JPG" by Kelovy · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Zlatni_rat_from_Vidova_Gora.JPG -->

entries.name: Bávaro Beach
entries.desc: An extraordinarily long Atlantic beach on the Dominican Republic's eastern coast, anchoring the Punta Cana resort zone, with fine white sand and clear, calm water sheltered by a coral reef.

photo.alt: Bávaro Beach's white sand and clear water, Punta Cana
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bavaro-beach-punta-cana.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Playa Bavaro.JPG" by Andreas Volkmer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Playa_Bavaro.JPG -->

entries.name: East Beach, Vomo Island
entries.desc: A soft white-sand beach on a small private island in Fiji's Mamanuca group, fronting a shallow coral lagoon with snorkeling directly off the shore.

photo.alt: Small private island in Fiji's Mamanuca group ringed by a shallow coral lagoon
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mamanuca-islands-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 DE · "Mamanuca island.jpg" by Isderion · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mamanuca_island.jpg -->

entries.name: Matira Beach
entries.desc: The main public beach on Bora Bora's southern tip, with soft sand, shallow water shelving gently into the lagoon, and a view of Mount Otemanu.

photo.alt: Matira Beach and lagoon, Bora Bora, French Polynesia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: matira-beach-bora-bora.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Matira Beach, Bora Bora, French Polynesia.jpg" by Scott Williams · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Matira_Beach,_Bora_Bora,_French_Polynesia.jpg -->

entries.name: Navagio Beach (Shipwreck Beach)
entries.desc: An isolated cove accessible only by boat, enclosed by tall limestone cliffs and named for the rusting hull of a smuggling ship beached on its white sand.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Navagio Beach's white sand cove, shipwreck, and limestone cliffs, Zakynthos
photo.credit: <!-- photo: navagio-beach-zakynthos.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Navagio Beach and Shipwreck of the Panagiotis at 'Smugglers Cove' Zakynthos.JPG" by Badgernet · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Navagio_Beach_and_Shipwreck_of_the_Panagiotis_at_%27Smugglers_Cove%27_Zakynthos.JPG -->

entries.name: Elafonissi Beach
entries.desc: A shallow lagoon beach on Crete's southwestern corner, where pale pink-tinged sand and a small islet are separated by wadeable, warm turquoise water.

photo.alt: Pink-tinged sand and shallow turquoise lagoon water at Elafonisi, Crete
photo.credit: <!-- photo: elafonisi-beach-crete.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Elafonisi beach.jpg" by Kostas Lotsos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Elafonisi_beach.jpg -->

entries.name: Nusa Penida (Kelingking Beach)
entries.desc: A remote cove on the island of Nusa Penida below a T-shaped limestone headland resembling a dinosaur, reached by a steep, exposed cliffside descent.

photo.alt: The T-shaped limestone headland and cove at Kelingking Beach, Nusa Penida
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kelingking-beach-nusa-penida.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Kelingking Beach, Nusa Penida.jpg" by Shrigusti · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kelingking_Beach,_Nusa_Penida.jpg -->

entries.name: Rabbit Beach (Spiaggia dei Conigli)
entries.desc: A protected beach on the island of Lampedusa reached by a downhill path, facing a small offshore islet and a loggerhead sea turtle nesting site.

photo.alt: Rabbit Beach's sand and turquoise water facing the offshore islet, Lampedusa
photo.credit: <!-- photo: rabbit-beach-lampedusa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Lampedusa Rabbit beach - panoramio.jpg" by Pier B. · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lampedusa_Rabbit_beach_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Cala Brandinchi
entries.desc: A shallow white-sand bay on Sardinia's northeastern coast, backed by pine woods, with gently shelving turquoise water often compared to Caribbean shallows.

photo.alt: White-sand bay and pine-backed turquoise shallows at Cala Brandinchi, Sardinia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cala-brandinchi-sardinia.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "San Teodoro - Sardinia - 2017 - Cala Brandinchi.JPG" by Benoît Prieur · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:San_Teodoro_-_Sardinia_-_2017_-_Cala_Brandinchi.JPG -->

entries.name: Diani Beach
entries.desc: A long white-coral-sand beach south of Mombasa on Kenya's Indian Ocean coast, backed by coastal woodland and fronted by a reef lagoon that keeps the water warm and calm for swimming.

photo.alt: White coral sand and reef-calmed water at Diani Beach, Kenya
photo.credit: <!-- photo: diani-beach-kenya.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Diani Beach Kenya.jpg" by User:Bkmiles · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Diani_Beach_Kenya.jpg -->

entries.name: Nosy Iranja
entries.desc: Two small islets off northwestern Madagascar joined at low tide by a wide white sandbar, surrounded by shallow, clear water and reached by boat.

photo.alt: The sandbar joining Nosy Iranja's two islets, surrounded by shallow clear water
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nosy-iranja-madagascar.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Nosy-iranja-beach.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nosy-iranja-beach.jpg -->

entries.name: Fulhadhoo Beach
entries.desc: A quiet white-sand beach on a small local island in the Maldives' Baa Atoll, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, fronting a shallow reef lagoon.

photo.alt: Aerial view of islands and turquoise reef in Baa Atoll, Maldives
photo.credit: <!-- photo: baa-atoll-aerial.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Baa atoll islands.JPG" by Frédéric Ducarme · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Baa_atoll_islands.JPG -->

entries.name: Trou aux Biches
entries.desc: A long white-sand beach on the northwest coast of Mauritius, lined with casuarina trees and fronting a calm reef-protected lagoon good for snorkeling.

photo.alt: Trou aux Biches beach and lagoon on Mauritius's northwest coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: trou-aux-biches-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Trou aux Biches Beach in Mauritius, a view from the north" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Trou_aux_Biches_Beach_in_Mauritius,_a_view_from_the_north_(53698229530).jpg -->

entries.name: Tulum Beach
entries.desc: A white-sand Caribbean beach on Mexico's Yucatán coast, where a cliff-top cluster of Maya ruins overlooks the turquoise water at the north end.

photo.alt: Beach below the Tulum Maya ruins on the Yucatán coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tulum-beach-ruins.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Beach and Ruins at Tulum" by FeldBum · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Beach_and_Ruins_at_Tulum.jpg -->

entries.name: Playa del Amor (Hidden Beach)
entries.desc: A hidden beach inside a collapsed crater on the uninhabited Marietas Islands, open to the sky but enclosed by rock, reached by swimming through a sea tunnel.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the Hidden Beach crater at the Marietas Islands
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hidden-beach-marietas.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Isla Marieta( isla del amor)" by Luis Carlos Soria Contreras · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Isla_Marieta(_isla_del_amor).jpg -->

entries.name: Playa Norte
entries.desc: A shallow beach at the northern tip of Isla Mujeres, off Cancún, with calm, waist-deep turquoise water extending well out from the soft white sand.

photo.alt: Shallow turquoise water at Playa Norte, Isla Mujeres
photo.credit: <!-- photo: playa-norte-isla-mujeres.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Playa Norte on Isla Mujeres, Mexico" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Playa_Norte_on_Isla_Mujeres,_Mexico_(41791715860).jpg -->

entries.name: Playa Balandra
entries.tag: La Paz, Baja California Sur, Mexico
entries.desc: A protected bay north of La Paz with extremely shallow, calm water in vivid blue-green shades, backed by desert mountains; accessed on foot with no resort development on the shore.

photo.alt: Balandra Bay's shallow blue-green water near La Paz
photo.credit: <!-- photo: playa-balandra.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Playa Balandra" by C.apperti · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Playa_Balandra.JPG -->

entries.name: Playa de los Muertos
entries.desc: The social heart of Puerto Vallarta's Zona Romántica, a wide sandy beach lined with palapa bars and beach clubs, backed by the colourful rooftops of the old town and the Sierra Madre.

photo.alt: Playa de los Muertos beach in Puerto Vallarta's Zona Romántica
photo.credit: <!-- photo: playa-de-los-muertos.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Playa de Los Muertos" by Melikamp · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Playa_de_Los_Muertos.jpg -->

entries.name: El Médano Beach
entries.tag: Cabo San Lucas, Baja California Sur, Mexico
entries.desc: The only swimmable beach in Cabo San Lucas, a broad arc of golden sand at the tip of the Baja Peninsula fronting the Sea of Cortez, with calmer surf than the Pacific-exposed shores.

photo.alt: El Médano Beach fronting the Sea of Cortez in Cabo San Lucas
photo.credit: <!-- photo: el-medano-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "El Medano, San Lucas, Baja California Sur" by Kirt Edblom · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:El_Medano,_San_Lucas,_Baja_California_Sur.jpg -->

entries.name: Entalula Beach
entries.desc: A small white-sand beach on an island in the Bacuit Archipelago near El Nido, ringed by dramatic limestone karst cliffs and reached by boat.

photo.alt: Limestone karst cliffs framing a small white-sand cove in the Bacuit Archipelago, El Nido
photo.credit: <!-- photo: entalula-island-el-nido.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Entalula Island, El Nido, Philippines.jpg" by Fabio Achilli · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entalula_Island,_El_Nido,_Philippines.jpg -->

entries.name: Benagil Sea Cave Beach
entries.desc: A small sand floor inside a sea cave on the Algarve coast, lit through a circular opening in the rock ceiling and reachable only by boat, kayak, or swimming.

photo.alt: Sunlight beaming through the sea cave's rock skylight onto the sand floor at Benagil
photo.credit: <!-- photo: benagil-sea-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "A Catedral de Benagil.JPG" by Evarusso · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_Catedral_de_Benagil.JPG -->

entries.name: Praia da Marinha
entries.desc: A cliff-backed Algarve beach framed by ochre limestone rock formations and sea arches, reached by a stairway down from the clifftop and a coastal trail.

photo.alt: Ochre limestone sea stacks and arches framing the sand at Praia da Marinha, Algarve
photo.credit: <!-- photo: praia-da-marinha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Praia da Marinha 2017.jpg" by Mimihitam · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Praia_da_Marinha_2017.jpg -->

entries.name: Flamenco Beach
entries.desc: A horseshoe bay on the island of Culebra, reached by ferry from Fajardo, with fine white sand and exceptionally calm, clear water set among low green hills.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the horseshoe-shaped bay and white sand at Flamenco Beach, Culebra
photo.credit: <!-- photo: flamenco-beach-culebra.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Flamenco Beach, Culebra Island, Puerto Rico.jpg" by Breezy Baldwin · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Flamenco_Beach,_Culebra_Island,_Puerto_Rico.jpg -->

entries.name: Colombier Beach
entries.desc: A secluded cove at the northwestern tip of St. Barthélemy, reached only by a coastal hiking trail or by boat, backed by dry scrub-covered hills.

photo.alt: Secluded cove backed by scrub-covered hills at Colombier Beach with sailboats anchored offshore
photo.credit: <!-- photo: colombier-beach-st-barts.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Colombier Beach (46845329694).jpg" by David Stanley · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Colombier_Beach_(46845329694).jpg -->

entries.name: Anse Source d'Argent
entries.desc: A shallow beach on the island of La Digue famous for its sculpted pink granite boulders, which frame stretches of soft white sand and clear, calm water.

photo.alt: Sculpted pink granite boulders framing white sand and a leaning palm at Anse Source d'Argent, La Digue
photo.credit: <!-- photo: anse-source-dargent.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Anse Source d'Argent 2-La Digue.jpg" by Tobias Alt · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Anse_Source_d%27Argent_2-La_Digue.jpg -->

entries.name: Anse Lazio
entries.desc: A curved white-sand bay on the island of Praslin, bracketed by granite headlands and forested hills, with clear water for swimming and snorkeling.

photo.alt: Granite boulders and forested hills framing the white-sand bay at Anse Lazio, Praslin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: anse-lazio-praslin.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Beach Anse Lazio, Praslin Seychelles (39587592322).jpg" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Beach_Anse_Lazio,_Praslin_Seychelles_(39587592322).jpg -->

entries.name: Maho Beach
entries.desc: A narrow beach at the end of Princess Juliana Airport's main runway, where arriving aircraft pass at extremely low altitude directly over swimmers on the sand.

photo.alt: An airliner passing at extremely low altitude over sunbathers on the sand at Maho Beach, Sint Maarten
photo.credit: <!-- photo: maho-beach-sxm.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Maho Beach, near Princess Juliana Airport, Caribbean island of Saint Martin-8Feb2008 (3).jpg" by alljengi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Maho_Beach,_near_Princess_Juliana_Airport,_Caribbean_island_of_Saint_Martin-8Feb2008_(3).jpg -->

entries.name: Boulders Beach
entries.tag: Simon's Town, Cape Town, South Africa
entries.desc: A sheltered beach of large granite boulders and small sandy coves near Simon's Town, home to a resident colony of African penguins on the shore.

photo.alt: Boulders Beach granite boulders with African penguins, Simon's Town
photo.credit: <!-- photo: boulders-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Boulders Beach (135941570).jpg" by Mike · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Boulders_Beach_(135941570).jpg -->

entries.name: Camps Bay
entries.desc: A white-sand beach beneath the Twelve Apostles mountain range in Cape Town, backed by a palm-lined promenade; the Atlantic water here stays notably cold.

photo.alt: Camps Bay beach with the Twelve Apostles mountains behind
photo.credit: <!-- photo: camps-bay-cape-town.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Camps Bay, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa.jpg" by landagent · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Camps_Bay,_Cape_Town,_8005,_South_Africa.jpg -->

entries.name: Cala Pregonda
entries.desc: A reddish-gold sand cove on Menorca's rugged north coast, reached on foot, framed by iron-rich rock formations and a jagged offshore islet.

photo.alt: Cala Pregonda's reddish sand cove and rock islet, Menorca
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cala-pregonda-menorca.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Cala Pregonda.jpg" by MontanNito · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cala_Pregonda.jpg -->

entries.name: Ses Illetes
entries.desc: A fine white-sand beach on a narrow spit at the north of Formentera, backed by dunes and pine, with shallow, exceptionally clear Mediterranean water.

photo.alt: Ses Illetes white sand beach and clear water, Formentera
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ses-illetes-formentera.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Formentera - Platja De Ses Illetes - panoramio (1).jpg" by MrPepanos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Formentera_-_Platja_De_Ses_Illetes_-_panoramio_(1).jpg -->

entries.name: Es Trenc
entries.desc: A long, largely undeveloped beach on Mallorca's south coast backed by dunes and scrub pine, one of the few stretches of the island untouched by resort construction.

photo.alt: Es Trenc undeveloped beach and dunes, Mallorca
photo.credit: <!-- photo: es-trenc-mallorca.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Es Trenc, playa, al fondo Sa Rápita.jpg" by JM92 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Es_Trenc,_playa,_al_fondo_Sa_R%C3%A1pita.jpg -->

entries.name: Unawatuna Beach
entries.desc: A curved bay on Sri Lanka's south coast near Galle, partly sheltered by a reef and headland, backed by palms and a cluster of beachfront guesthouses.

photo.alt: Wijaya Beach at Unawatuna, palm-backed shore, Sri Lanka
photo.credit: <!-- photo: unawatuna-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Wijaya beach Unawatuna Sri Lanka.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wijaya_beach_Unawatuna_Sri_Lanka.jpg -->

entries.name: Nungwi Beach
entries.desc: A beach at Zanzibar's northern tip where, unlike much of the island, the tidal range is small enough for swimming through the day; a hub for dhow sailing and diving.

photo.alt: Nungwi Beach white sand shore, Zanzibar
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nungwi-beach-zanzibar.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "White sandy beach at Nungwi, Zanzibar.jpg" by ObonyoML · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:White_sandy_beach_at_Nungwi,_Zanzibar.jpg -->

entries.name: Railay Beach
entries.desc: A peninsula in Krabi accessible only by boat, cut off from the mainland by towering limestone cliffs that draw rock climbers to its sandy, palm-backed shore.

photo.alt: Railay Beach limestone cliffs and sandy shore, Krabi
photo.credit: <!-- photo: railay-beach-krabi.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Railay Beach at Sunrise.jpg" by Mark Fischer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Railay_Beach_at_Sunrise.jpg -->

entries.name: Koh Lipe (Sunrise Beach)
entries.desc: The eastern beach of a small island in the Andaman Sea near the Malaysian border, with fine white sand, clear water, and coral reefs offshore.

photo.alt: White sand spit and turquoise water on Koh Lipe, Satun, Thailand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: koh-lipe.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Koh Lipe island coast" by VascoPlanet World Photography · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Koh_Lipe_island_coast.jpg -->

entries.name: Maya Bay
entries.desc: A sheltered cove on the island of Phi Phi Leh, enclosed by steep limestone cliffs and made famous by a film; access is managed to protect its recovering reef.

photo.alt: Maya Bay cove enclosed by limestone cliffs, Phi Phi Leh, Thailand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: maya-bay-phi-phi-leh.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Playa Maya, Ko Phi Phi, Tailandia, 2013-08-19, DD 17" by Diego Delso · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Playa_Maya,_Ko_Phi_Phi,_Tailandia,_2013-08-19,_DD_17.JPG -->

entries.name: Ölüdeniz (Blue Lagoon)
entries.desc: A sheltered lagoon on Turkey's southwestern Turquoise Coast, where a sandbar encloses still, vivid blue-green water beneath pine-covered mountains and paragliding launch sites.

photo.alt: Sandbar-enclosed blue-green lagoon beneath pine-covered mountains at Ölüdeniz, Turkey
photo.credit: <!-- photo: blue-lagoon-oludeniz.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Oludeniz – Blue lagoon" by Jongleur100 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Oludeniz_%E2%80%93_Blue_lagoon.jpg -->

entries.name: Grace Bay Beach
entries.desc: A long stretch of powder-white sand along the north shore of Providenciales, fronting a calm turquoise lagoon sheltered by a barrier reef offshore.

photo.alt: Sunset over Grace Bay's white sand and calm water, Providenciales, Turks and Caicos
photo.credit: <!-- photo: grace-bay.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Sunset in Grace Bay, Turks and Caicos Islands" by Jason Boldero · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sunset_in_Grace_Bay,_Turks_and_Caicos_Islands.jpg -->

entries.name: Kynance Cove
entries.desc: A small cove on Cornwall's Lizard Peninsula exposing dramatic serpentine rock stacks at low tide, with unusually clear turquoise water for the British Atlantic coast.

photo.alt: Serpentine rock stacks and sandy cove at Kynance Cove, Lizard Peninsula, Cornwall
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kynance-cove.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Kynance 087" by Tommytrouble at English Wikipedia · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kynance_087.jpg -->

entries.name: Lanikai Beach
entries.desc: A soft white-sand residential beach on Oahu's windward coast, fronting calm, shallow water and looking out to the twin Mokulua islets offshore.

photo.alt: White sand beach facing the twin Mokulua islets, Lanikai, Oahu
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lanikai-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Lanikai Beach, Hawaii" by Cristo Vlahos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lanikai_Beach,_Hawaii.JPG -->

entries.name: Kailua Beach
entries.desc: A wide crescent of powdery white sand on Oahu's windward coast, with consistent trade winds that make it one of the best kiteboarding and windsurfing beaches in the state.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Kailua town and the turquoise waters of Kailua Bay, Oahu
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kailua-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Kailua Oahu" by MercolaOverMerck at English Wikipedia · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kailua_Oahu.JPG -->

entries.name: Waimea Bay Beach Park
entries.desc: A wide bay on Oahu's North Shore, calm and ideal for swimming in summer, then home to some of the largest rideable waves in the world when the winter swells arrive.

photo.alt: Large winter swells breaking at Waimea Bay, North Shore, Oahu
photo.credit: <!-- photo: waimea-bay.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Waimea Bay, Oahu, Hawaii - panoramio" by Steve Hedin · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Waimea_Bay,_Oahu,_Hawaii_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Ka'anapali Beach
entries.desc: A long golden-sand resort beach on West Maui, backed by hotels and a shoreline path, with a rocky point at Black Rock popular for snorkeling.

photo.alt: Kaanapali Beach with resort hotels and the rocky Black Rock point at Maui
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kaanapali-beach-maui.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Black Rock beach Kaanapali Maui Hawaii" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Black_Rock_beach_Kaanapali_Maui_Hawaii_(30800035877).jpg -->

entries.name: Hamoa Beach
entries.desc: A remote half-moon bay on Maui's eastern Hana coast, reached by a winding road, with grey-sand fringed by sea cliffs and surf that draws bodysurfers.

photo.alt: Hamoa Beach's half-moon bay on Maui's Hana coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hamoa-beach-maui.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "HamoaBeach" by navin75 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:HamoaBeach.jpg -->

entries.name: Big Beach (Oneloa)
entries.desc: A large, largely undeveloped beach of white sand in Makena State Park on South Maui, known for its bodyboarding shore break and the small rocky cinder cone at its southern end.

photo.alt: Big Beach (Oneloa) in Makena State Park with the cinder cone at its southern end
photo.credit: <!-- photo: big-beach-oneloa-maui.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Hawaii Maui Makena Big Beach (cropped)" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hawaii_Maui_Makena_Big_Beach_(22649774315)_(cropped).jpg -->

entries.name: Waianapanapa Black Sand Beach
entries.desc: A jet-black volcanic pebble beach in Waianapanapa State Park near Hana, surrounded by dramatic lava sea arches, caves, and a freshwater spring fed from the cliffs.

photo.alt: Jet-black volcanic pebble cove at Waianapanapa State Park near Hana
photo.credit: <!-- photo: waianapanapa-black-sand-beach-maui.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Waianapanapa black sand beach Maui Hawaii Road to Hana" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Waianapanapa_black_sand_beach_Maui_Hawaii_Road_to_Hana_(43923399650).jpg -->

entries.name: Hapuna Beach
entries.desc: A wide sweep of white sand on the Big Island's Kohala Coast, consistently ranked among the finest beaches in Hawaii, with clear water for swimming and snorkeling and strong shore break in winter.

photo.alt: Wide sweep of white sand at Hapuna Beach on the Big Island's Kohala Coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hapuna-beach-big-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Hapuna Beach 2017" by Ekrem Canli · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hapuna_Beach_2017.jpg -->

entries.name: Punalu'u Black Sand Beach
entries.desc: A black volcanic-sand beach on the Big Island's southeastern coast, fringed by coconut palms and frequented by green sea turtles that rest on the shore.

photo.alt: Green sea turtle basking on the black volcanic sand at Punalu'u Beach, palms behind
photo.credit: <!-- photo: punaluu-black-sand-beach-big-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Green turtle Chelonia mydas is basking on Punaluu Beach Big Island of Hawaii" by Brocken Inaglory · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Green_turtle_Chelonia_mydas_is_basking_on_Punaluu_Beach_Big_Island_of_Hawaii.jpg -->

entries.name: Papakolea Green Sand Beach
entries.desc: A small beach in a collapsed volcanic cinder cone on the Big Island's Ka Lae coast, where the sand is tinted olive-green by olivine crystals eroded from the surrounding lava walls.

photo.alt: Olive-green sand cove inside the collapsed cinder cone at Papakolea, South Point
photo.credit: <!-- photo: papakolea-green-sand-beach-big-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Panorama of Papakolea green sand beach in Hawaii" by Aren Elliott · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Panorama_of_Papakolea_green_sand_beach_in_Hawaii.jpg -->

meta: One of Two Green Sand Beaches Worldwide

entries.name: Hanalei Bay
entries.desc: A wide crescent bay on Kauai's north shore backed by the fluted green ridges of the Na Pali coast, with calm water for swimming in summer and large surf in winter.

photo.alt: Hanalei Bay and pier on Kauai's north shore backed by green mountain ridges
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hanalei-bay-kauai.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Hanalei Pier Kauai" by Ralf Beier · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hanalei_Pier_Kauai.jpg -->

entries.name: Ke'e Beach
entries.desc: A small reef-sheltered beach at the end of Kauai's North Shore road, at the trailhead of the Na Pali Coast State Wilderness Park, with snorkeling inside the reef when seas are calm.

photo.alt: Kee Beach at the end of the Kalalau Trail, Kauai
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kee-beach-kauai.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "2006-08-02 - Kee Beach from Kalalau Trail.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:2006-08-02_-_Kee_Beach_from_Kalalau_Trail.jpg -->

entries.name: Polihale Beach
entries.desc: A remote, undeveloped beach at the western end of the Na Pali cliffs, reached by a dirt road across sugar-cane fields, with broad dunes and dramatic views of the sea cliffs above.

photo.alt: Polihale Beach dunes below the Na Pali sea cliffs, Kauai
photo.credit: <!-- photo: polihale-beach-kauai.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Polihale Strand Kauai, Hawaii (45365274715)" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Polihale_Strand_Kauai,_Hawaii_(45365274715).jpg -->

entries.name: Hulopoe Beach
entries.desc: A crescent of white sand in a marine life conservation district on Lanai's southern coast, with spinner dolphins frequently in the bay, tide pools at the rocky headland, and snorkeling over healthy coral.

photo.alt: Puu Pehe (Sweetheart Rock), the rocky headland at Hulopoe Beach, Lanai
photo.credit: <!-- photo: puu-pehe-hulopoe-lanai.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Lanai-Puupehe-silhouette-from-ManeleBay" by Joel Bradshaw · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lanai-Puupehe-silhouette-from-ManeleBay.JPG -->

entries.name: Papohaku Beach
entries.desc: A broad, undeveloped golden-sand beach on Molokai's west end, often nearly empty, with strong surf and currents that make it better for walking than swimming.

photo.alt: Papohaku Beach's broad golden sand on Molokai's west end
photo.credit: <!-- photo: papohaku-beach-molokai.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Papohaku Beach Park, Maunaloa" by Bob Linsdell · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Papohaku_Beach_Park,_Maunaloa_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Siesta Key Beach
entries.desc: A wide Gulf of Mexico beach on a barrier island near Sarasota, known for its cool, powdery quartz sand that stays soft underfoot and calm, gently sloping water.

photo.alt: Siesta Key Beach's powdery quartz sand near Sarasota
photo.credit: <!-- photo: siesta-key-beach-sarasota.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Siesta Beach.jpg" by Justicemtran · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Siesta_Beach.jpg -->

entries.name: Clearwater Beach
entries.desc: A lively Gulf-coast barrier-island beach west of Tampa, with wide white sand, a pedestrian promenade, and a marina district at its northern end.

photo.alt: Clearwater Beach's wide white sand on the Gulf coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: clearwater-beach-florida.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Clearwater Beach, Florida (35188147390)" by Michel Rathwell · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Clearwater_Beach,_Florida_(35188147390).jpg -->

entries.name: La Jolla Cove
entries.desc: A small protected cove framed by sandstone bluffs in La Jolla, part of an ecological reserve, with clear water popular for swimming and snorkeling among sea life.

photo.alt: La Jolla Cove framed by sandstone bluffs, San Diego
photo.credit: <!-- photo: la-jolla-cove-san-diego.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "La Jolla Cove, San Diego, California (9) (5141541857)" by Ken Lund · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:La_Jolla_Cove,_San_Diego,_California_(9)_(5141541857).jpg -->

entries.name: El Matador Beach
entries.desc: A Malibu state beach reached by a stairway down the bluff, known for its sea caves, rock arches, and weathered sandstone sea stacks rising from the sand.

photo.alt: El Matador Beach's sea stacks and rock arches, Malibu
photo.credit: <!-- photo: el-matador-beach-malibu.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "El matador beach malibu" by Graham · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:El_matador_beach_malibu.jpg -->

entries.name: Pfeiffer Beach
entries.desc: A Big Sur beach reached by an unmarked side road, known for purple-tinged manganese-garnet sand and a rock arch through which surf and light break at sunset.

photo.alt: Pfeiffer Beach's purple-tinged sand and rock arch at sunset, Big Sur
photo.credit: <!-- photo: pfeiffer-beach-purple-sand.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Pfeiffer Beach, Big Sur, California, United States.jpg" by Robert Brett · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pfeiffer_Beach,_Big_Sur,_California,_United_States.jpg -->

entries.name: Trunk Bay
entries.desc: A small crescent bay within Virgin Islands National Park, backed by forested hills and known for an offshore underwater snorkeling trail marked among the coral.

photo.alt: Trunk Bay's crescent shoreline seen from the overlook, St. John
photo.credit: <!-- photo: trunk-bay-overlook.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Trunk Bay from overlook, St. John, USVI" by Gruepig · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:TrunkOverlook.jpg -->
